name: Political Mapping
summary: A strategic technique for understanding who supports, resists, or influences a deal behind the scenes and why.
whenToUseIt: Use political mapping in enterprise and strategic account deals where stakeholder agendas differ and internal alignment cannot be assumed.
steps: Identify the visible and hidden stakeholders with influence over the decision.
Assess who is supportive, neutral, skeptical, or opposed and what drives that position.
Adjust your stakeholder strategy so proof and messaging match the political reality of the account.
example: Operations wants the change, IT is cautious, finance is skeptical on spend, and a legacy system owner feels threatened. Political mapping stops the rep from assuming the enthusiastic champion represents the whole account.
